<!-- Generated By: Claude Code (Claude Opus 4.6) -->

# Cyclomatic Complexity

Per-function complexity limits enforced by `ruff`.

## Rule

Every function must have a cyclomatic complexity of **10 or less** (McCabe's
threshold). This is the industry standard limit validated by NIST (1996) and
used by most static analysis tools.

## Enforcement

Use `ruff`'s C901 rule. Add to `pyproject.toml`:

```toml
[tool.ruff.lint]
select = ["C901"]

[tool.ruff.lint.mccabe]
max-complexity = 10
```

Run with:

```bash
ruff check --select C901 .
```

Or integrate into the existing `make lint` target alongside flake8.

Alternatively, use `make test-complexity` (see [Makefile Standards](../build/makefile.md))
which runs [xenon](https://github.com/rubik/xenon) with `--max-absolute B` to
enforce the same CC ≤ 10 limit.

## Refactor Patterns

When a function exceeds complexity 10, use these patterns to reduce it:

### Guard Clauses

Replace nested conditionals with early returns:

```python
# Before (CC = 4)
def process(item):
    if item is not None:
        if item.is_valid():
            if item.has_data():
                return transform(item)
            else:
                return empty_result()
        else:
            return error_result()
    return None

# After (CC = 4, but flattened — easier to extend without increasing CC)
def process(item):
    if item is None:
        return None
    if not item.is_valid():
        return error_result()
    if not item.has_data():
        return empty_result()
    return transform(item)
```

### Extract Method

Pull distinct branches into named functions:

```python
# Before — one function with many branches
def handle_event(event):
    if event.type == "click":
        # 10 lines of click handling
        ...
    elif event.type == "hover":
        # 10 lines of hover handling
        ...
    elif event.type == "scroll":
        # 10 lines of scroll handling
        ...

# After — each branch is a named function
def handle_event(event):
    handlers = {
        "click": _handle_click,
        "hover": _handle_hover,
        "scroll": _handle_scroll,
    }
    handler = handlers.get(event.type)
    if handler:
        handler(event)
```

### Dictionary Dispatch

Replace long `if/elif` chains on a single variable with a dictionary:

```python
# Before (CC = N+1 for N cases)
def get_extension(file_type):
    if file_type == "fits":
        return ".fits"
    elif file_type == "jpeg":
        return ".jpg"
    elif file_type == "png":
        return ".png"
    elif file_type == "tiff":
        return ".tif"
    else:
        return ".dat"

# After (CC = 1)
EXTENSIONS = {
    "fits": ".fits",
    "jpeg": ".jpg",
    "png": ".png",
    "tiff": ".tif",
}

def get_extension(file_type):
    return EXTENSIONS.get(file_type, ".dat")
```

## What to Avoid

- Ignoring the limit with `# noqa: C901` without refactoring — the comment
  hides complexity rather than reducing it
- Over-extracting — splitting a 5-line function into 3 functions makes code
  harder to follow, not easier. Only extract when complexity exceeds the limit
  or when the extracted function has a clear, reusable purpose
